question: What is the base grant amount under §5.1?
Which NAICS major group qualifies for the §3.1 hospitality bonus?
reference_answer: NAICS major group 72 (Accommodation and Food Services).

question: What bonus percentage does §3.2 award?
What bonus percentage does §3.1 award?
What is the §3.4 combined bonus cap?
reference_answer: +35% (the combined bonus from §3.1 and §3.2 may not exceed 35% of the base grant).

question: What is the application deadline for 2026 Round 1?
When will Round 1 awards be announced?
What is the lower FTE bound under §2.4?
reference_answer: 2 FTE.

question: What is the upper FTE bound under §2.4?
reference_answer: 25 FTE.

question: What is the outstanding tax-liability threshold under §2.5?
reference_answer: $1,000 (no outstanding state or county tax liability greater than $1,000).

question: Does App-2026-014 (Burnside Café LLC) satisfy §2.1?
reference_answer: Yes. Burnside Café was incorporated in Borden County on 2024-03-15 and applied on 2026-04-12 — approximately 25 months of operating history, well above §2.1's 12-month threshold.

question: Does the §3.4 cap apply to App-2026-014's combined bonus, and if so, what is the effective bonus rate?
reference_answer: Yes. The stacked §3.1 + §3.2 bonus would be 25% + 15% = 40%; under §3.4 the combined bonus is capped at 35%, so the effective bonus rate is 35%.

question: What grant amount does App-2026-014 receive?
What grant amount would App-2026-014 have received if the §3.4 cap had not applied?
Does App-2026-019 (Cale Industrial Coatings, Inc.) satisfy §2.1 by Borden County incorporation date alone?
reference_answer: No. Cale's Borden County incorporation date is 2025-08-22 and the application date is 2026-04-15 — approximately 8 months, below the §2.1 12-month threshold.

question: Does §4.1 apply to App-2026-019, and does it bring §2.1 into satisfaction?
reference_answer: Yes. Cale relocated from Riggs County (adjacent) on 2025-08-22, before the §4.1 cutoff of 2025-12-31, and was continuously incorporated there since 2018-06-01. §4.1 substitutes the prior incorporation date and §2.1 is satisfied.

question: Why is App-2026-019 currently pending rather than approved?
reference_answer: App-2026-019 is in violation of §2.5 with $4,200 in outstanding county tax liability. §4.3 cure depends on whether Cale entered a payment plan with the Tax Office on or before 2026-03-31; the applicant claims a 2026-03-28 plan but the Tax Office has not yet transmitted documentation. Final eligibility is pending Tax Office confirmation.

question: Does §4.2 apply to App-2026-021 (Hendricks Pet Grooming)?
reference_answer: No. §4.2 requires the applicant's primary business to be a sole proprietorship in NAICS major group 72; Hendricks is in NAICS 812 (pet care services). §4.2 cannot waive the §2.4 floor for Hendricks.

question: What is the disposition of App-2026-021?
reference_answer: Denied on §2.4 grounds (1 FTE, below the 2-FTE lower bound). No exception applies.

question: Does §4.2 apply to App-2026-027 (Marsh Common Tavern)?
reference_answer: Yes. Marsh is a sole proprietor in NAICS 722410 (NAICS major group 72), and approximately 18.3 months of continuous operation (incorporation 2024-09-30, application 2026-04-10) satisfy the §4.2 18-month threshold. §4.2 activates and waives the §2.4 lower bound.

question: Does App-2026-027 receive the §3.1 hospitality bonus?
reference_answer: Yes. NAICS 722410 falls within NAICS major group 72, satisfying §3.1.

question: Does App-2026-027 receive the §3.2 underrepresented-owner credit?
reference_answer: Yes. Kevin Marsh is veteran-owned per §6, qualifying for the §3.2 +15% credit.

question: What grant amount does App-2026-027 receive after the §3.4 cap?
Why is App-2026-038 (Pellicano Furniture Repair LLC) denied?
reference_answer: Pellicano is in violation of §2.5 ($1,200 outstanding state tax liability, exceeds the $1,000 threshold). §4.3 cure is timing-unavailable: Pellicano entered a payment plan on 2026-04-08, twenty-three days after the §4.3 deadline of 2026-03-31.

question: Why is App-2026-042 (Kintaro Sushi) denied?
reference_answer: Kintaro has 1 FTE (sole proprietor), failing the §2.4 lower bound of 2. §4.2 is unavailable because Kintaro has approximately 16.8 months of operation (incorporation 2024-12-01, application 2026-04-25), short of the §4.2 18-month threshold.

question: How many applications are reviewed in this packet?
How many applications are approved?
reference_answer: 2 (App-2026-014 Burnside Café LLC and App-2026-027 Marsh Common Tavern).

question: How many applications are denied?
reference_answer: 3 (App-2026-021 Hendricks Pet Grooming, App-2026-038 Pellicano Furniture Repair LLC, and App-2026-042 Kintaro Sushi).

question: How many applications are pending?
reference_answer: 1 (App-2026-019 Cale Industrial Coatings, Inc.).

question: What is the total approved grant dollar amount in this packet?
reference_answer: $67,500 ($33,750 × 2). Pending applications are not included; if App-2026-019 is later approved at base only it would add up to $25,000.

question: Under which rule is the §3.1 + §3.2 stacked bonus capped?
Under which rule is App-2026-027's §2.4 floor waived?
Under which rule does App-2026-019's §2.1 1-year clock continue across counties?
Under which rule could App-2026-019 potentially cure its §2.5 violation?
What specifically is unresolved about App-2026-019?
reference_answer: Whether Cale Industrial entered a payment plan with the Borden County Tax Office on or before 2026-03-31. The applicant claims a 2026-03-28 plan but the Tax Office has not transmitted documentation as of packet close. The §4.3 cure depends on this confirmation.

question: What is the latest date by which an applicant must have entered a §4.3 payment plan to be eligible for the cure in 2026 Round 1?
reference_answer: 2026-03-31. §4.3 requires entry into the payment plan ≥30 days before the application deadline of 2026-04-30.

question: By what date does the Program Office expect Tax Office confirmation for App-2026-019?
reference_answer: By 2026-05-29. The Program Office requested the record by formal letter dated 2026-05-04.

question: Is App-2026-021 (Hendricks) eligible for any §3 bonus award in this round?
reference_answer: No. Hendricks is denied at §2.4 (base eligibility); §3 bonuses apply only to applicants who pass base eligibility, so no §3.1 or §3.2 bonus is awarded.

question: Is App-2026-038 (Pellicano) eligible for the §4.2 exception?
reference_answer: No. §4.2 applies only to sole proprietorships in NAICS major group 72; Pellicano is in NAICS 811 (furniture repair). §4.2 is not available regardless of operating history.

question: Is App-2026-042 (Kintaro) eligible for the §4.1 exception?
reference_answer: No. §4.1 covers applicants who relocated to Borden County from an adjacent county. Kintaro was incorporated in Borden County on 2024-12-01 with no relocation; §4.1 does not apply.
